dag.dag_name: Finance GL close CPU saturation on a co-located worker

tasks.dependencies: Estimates peak resource usage from row-count metadata before compute
Resource-intensive in-memory computation, historically prone to exhaustion
Checks actual peak usage against budget mid-run/post-run
Branch: resource usage within safe budget
Branch: near resource ceiling, reroutes to chunked processing
Logs final resource profile for trend monitoring

incident.incident_name: Finance GL close CPU saturation on a co-located worker
incident.description: Resource Exhaustion incident on the Promotions pipeline (RTL_PRM_RES): finance GL close CPU saturation on a co-located worker.
incident.symptoms: The month-end GL elimination and consolidation tasks saturate their worker's CPU to 100% for the full 3-hour close window, delaying two co-scheduled unrelated DAGs on the same node.
incident.root_cause: Kubernetes node affinity rules were not configured, so this CPU-intensive, non-parallelized SQL-heavy task and two other CPU-bound DAGs were scheduled onto the same node by chance, and none of the three declared CPU resource requests/limits.
incident.business_impact: Two unrelated DAGs (Marketing reporting, Store Sales rollup) experienced execution slowdowns purely from CPU contention with the Finance close job, an entirely avoidable cross-domain impact.
incident.resolution: Set explicit CPU requests/limits on all three DAGs' pod templates; add anti-affinity rules keeping known CPU-heavy DAGs off the same node during overlapping windows.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: Kubernetes node affinity rules were not configured, so this CPU-intensive, non-parallelized SQL-heavy task and two other CPU-bound DAGs were scheduled onto the same node by chance, and none of the three declared CPU resource requests/limits.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow shows correlated slowdowns across three seemingly-unrelated DAGs, resolved by cross-referencing Kubernetes node placement
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog kubernetes.node.cpu.usage shows all three DAGs' pods co-located and collectively saturating the node
expected_rca.resolution: Set explicit CPU requests/limits on all three DAGs' pod templates; add anti-affinity rules keeping known CPU-heavy DAGs off the same node during overlapping windows.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Require every DAG in the metadata model to declare expected CPU/memory resource requests, used both for Kubernetes scheduling and for automatic anti-affinity rule generation.
